title: Riddles Of Merlin
content:
As I was walking
        Alone by the sea,
    "What is that whisper?"
        Said Merlin to me.
    "Only," I answered,
        "The sigh of the wave"--
    "Oh, no," replied Merlin,
        "'Tis the grass on your grave."

    As I lay dreaming
        In churchyard ground
    "Listen," said Merlin,
        "What is that sound?"
    "The green grass is growing,"
        I answered; but he
    Chuckled, "Oh, no!
        'Tis the sound of the sea."

    As I went homeward
        At dusk by the shore,
    "What is that crimson?"
        Said Merlin once more.
    "Only the sun," I said.
    "Sinking to rest"--
    "Sunset for East," he said,
        "Sunrise for West."
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Alfred Noyes delivers a powerful performance in "Riddles Of Merlin"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
